rows.definition: exec.CommandContext is the Go standard-library constructor that threads a cancellable context.Context into a spawned subprocess, one of the stdlib context-plumbing helpers fak uses to carry deadlines and cancellation through calls.
rows.distinction: Not the fak context-management plane (context-MMU, recall, ctxplan); these are Go-runtime cancellation/deadline plumbing, unrelated to model-visible context bytes.

rows.definition: syspromptmmu.BaseContextPlan returns the embedded-PromptSegment projection of the base system context (TierSpine then the TierPolicy floor) as a byte-identical, no-wire-mutation plan.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan context planner that materializes a turn's history view (ctxplan); BaseContextPlan only projects the fixed system-prompt spine into prompt segments.

rows.definition: compute.AutoSizeContextPlan derives the largest context-token count that fits available memory and returns the per-context KV/scratch MemoryPlan for it, reading geometry from a compute.ContextSizeConfig (KV/scratch configs plus the model's declared MaxContext).
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan turn-history planner (ctxplan); this sizes the KV/scratch memory budget at serve time and emits a MemoryPlan, not a model-visible context view.

rows.definition: putCtx is the context.Context carried through the modelengine PipelineEngine.run / lifecycle adapter for one put (decode) request, scoping cancellation of that pipeline step.
rows.distinction: Not the model-visible context plane (context-MMU, ctxplan); putCtx is per-request engine cancellation plumbing on the decode pipeline.

rows.definition: ctxParts is the local string slice that the Slack-post render functions (blockerpost, cachevaluepost, milestonepost, marketing) accumulate into a single Slack context block's text (ref, posted-by, etc.).
rows.distinction: Not the model-context plane; ctxParts assembles human-facing text for a Slack message's context block, unrelated to recall or KV context.

rows.definition: slackmeta.AppendContext appends a Slack context block carrying the signal-to-noise line to a message's blocks, unless an existing block already includes one.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxParts text builder for a single block (ctxParts (Slack post builder)) and not the model-context plane; AppendContext adds a whole S/N context block to a Slack post.

rows.definition: dos_recall is the DOS MCP tool (named across loopmap/rehydrate/accounts comments as the kernel-side equivalent) that re-verifies a recalled memory against the tree at read time before it is trusted, since a memory reflects only what was true when written.
rows.distinction: Not fak's own recall core-dump subsystem (recall); dos_recall is the external DOS re-checkable-fact tool for re-verifying recalled memory, not fak's persisted session image.

rows.definition: maxCtx is the local running maximum the ctxdemo / longctxbench / sessionbench tools track as they scan scenarios for the largest context length any scenario reaches.
rows.distinction: Not the model's fixed context-window limit (context window); maxCtx is a measured per-run peak context length accumulated in a demo/bench loop.

rows.definition: tombstoned_by_context_control is the ctxplan/contextq/memq refusal reason string returned when a span is denied page-in because it was suppressed by a context-control tombstone (recall.ErrTombstoned).
rows.distinction: Not the recall apply-side ContextActionTombstone that records the suppression; this is the read-side refusal reason emitted when a tombstoned page is skipped during materialization.

rows.definition: BUDGET_CONTEXT_EXHAUSTED is the session/gateway reset reason constant (session.ReasonBudgetContext, gateway.sessionReasonBudgetContext) emitted when ContextTokensLeft hits zero, returning a managed reset directive.
rows.distinction: Not the live remaining budget field (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); this is the closed reason-code string a session emits at the moment that budget is exhausted.

rows.definition: CompactionBudget is the gateway exit-summary field (Config.CompactHistoryBudget) holding the resident-token threshold the history rewrite fires past (0 means the compaction lever is OFF and the body is forwarded byte-for-byte).
rows.distinction: Not the compaction mechanism itself (compaction); CompactionBudget is the operator threshold knob, and CompactionShedTokens (aliased here) is the input-token count actually shed under it.

rows.definition: ContextBudget is the cmd/fak TUI report field (json context_budget) carrying a session's configured context-token budget for display in the overview/sessions panes.
rows.distinction: Not the runtime remaining count (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); ContextBudget is the configured budget surfaced in the console report shape.

rows.definition: CtxWith and CtxWithout are the cmd/tokendemo per-scenario counters of the model tokens added to context with fak's gate engaged (a refused/cached path) versus without it (the tool re-runs and its full result lands), used to show fak only saves on a refused bad call or a re-read.
rows.distinction: Not a runtime budget; CtxWith/CtxWithout are paired demo measurements of model tokens added to context under the two arms.

rows.definition: contextq.SkillContextRecord is the procedural-memory descriptor for one skill invocation, naming the producer and view-cache producer for that skill's context entry.
rows.distinction: Not the contextq materializer engine broadly (contextq); SkillContextRecord is a single skill-invocation descriptor row consumed by it.

rows.definition: ContextBench is an external academic code-agent context-retrieval benchmark (separate repo, Nanjing University + UCL research) named in docs/benchmarking/README.md as a context-retrieval comparison surface.
rows.distinction: Not fak's in-tree security-gate corpus benchmark (ctxbench (security-gate corpus cmd)); ContextBench is a third-party academic retrieval benchmark, not a fak command.

rows.definition: ContextCite is an external 2024 research method (Cohen-Wang et al., "ContextCite: Attributing Model Generation to Context") cited in fak's span-credit research notes as prior art for attributing generation to context spans.
rows.distinction: Not a fak subsystem; ContextCite is a cited third-party attribution method, distinct from fak's own recall/context machinery.

rows.definition: The derived ContextView is the cachemeta materialization view (exercised by TestFromMemoryViewDescribesDerivedContextView) that FromContextPage / FromMemoryView describes for a ContextPage span when deciding cache serveability.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan planned turn-history View; this is cachemeta's per-span materialization view used by the cache-serve gate.

rows.definition: ctxtape is the portable context-tape renderer (shipped in v0.33.0) that renders any trajectory, RSI, or education run as a portable context tape.
rows.distinction: Not a context PLANNER or security-gate bench (ctxplan, ctxbench); ctxtape is a presentation renderer that turns a trajectory into a portable tape artifact.

rows.definition: renderer is the pkg/scorecard term for the Jekyll-front-matter markdown projector half of a scorecard's shape (raw shape, control-pane JSON fold, markdown renderer, and the CLI), turning a scored corpus into a published doc.
rows.distinction: Not a model-context renderer (RenderPlan, Rendered); this renderer projects a scorecard corpus into Jekyll markdown for publishing.

rows.definition: agent.renderChatMLTools renders the message history plus tool schemas as Qwen2.5 canonical <tool_call>/<tool_response> ChatML and terminates with an open assistant turn for generation, delegating to renderTranscriptTools; renderTranscript is the zero-tools, no-open-turn form used by the poison-eviction path.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan span-paging Rendered view; these render an agent message history into the literal ChatML prompt string handed to the model.

rows.definition: agentdemo.Transcript.RenderText writes a plain-text walkthrough of an agent loop to a writer (the prompt, one line per step, the outcome), the human view of a demo transcript.
rows.distinction: Not the ChatML prompt renderers (renderChatMLTools) that build a model prompt; RenderText emits a human-readable demo walkthrough.

rows.definition: gateway Server.renderTurnDebugError emits one per-turn --debug-stats line for a FAILED turn (the trace, wire, error, and elapsed wall-clock), the failure-path counterpart that renderTurnDebugStats (which fires only on success) does not cover.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; this formats a single failed-turn debug telemetry line for the gateway's debug-stats sink.

rows.definition: sessionobs.RenderContrast writes the value-vs-waste session-observation contrast report to a writer (value/waste counts, separability, and per-feature discriminators ranked worst-first).
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderContrast emits the session-obs learn contrast view, not a benchmark comparison.

rows.definition: The renderSmoke* family writes the shared markdown sections of a SWE-bench smoke contract (renderSmokeArmsTable the Arm/Harness/Model table, renderSmokeGatesTable the Gate/OK/Detail table, renderSmokeEvidenceLink the compare-evidence-link block, renderRequiredBeforeClaim the required-before-any-result-claim bullet list).
rows.distinction: Not the post-run measured RenderMarkdown report; these render the pre-run smoke CONTRACT sections (the credentialed promise) before any number exists.

rows.definition: cmd/fak renderAutoDecision writes the `fak c --auto` ranked-decision table: cost-ordered compute targets, each with witnessed health/fit and an honestly-labeled quota stub, the winner, and the policy line.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; renderAutoDecision prints the compute-auto target ranking for the CLI, with [stub]/[n/a] tags so no placeholder reads as truth.

rows.definition: dojo.RenderBoard produces the human cross-lever calibration leaderboard table (worst-first) from a dojo Board.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderBoard renders the dojo calibration leaderboard, not a benchmark result document.

rows.definition: cmd/fak renderCommitResult prints the outcome of a safecommit run (the short SHA, path count, push suffix, and review), while renderPreview prints the `fak commit --preview` lint verdict (OK when the subject is witness-gradeable and bindable, else the blocking issues).
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; these render the human result/preview surfaces of the commit CLI verb.

rows.definition: milestonereport.RenderDist renders the M0..M7 maturity-rung distribution as a compact one-liner ("M0:2 M1:0 ...") in ladder order, exported so the Slack milestone card renders the same ladder line.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderDist emits the milestone-distribution one-liner over the support-maturity rungs.

rows.definition: cmd/fak renderGuardInfoPaneDryRun resolves the `fak guard --split` plan and renders it WITHOUT spawning anything (the multiplexer, the 80/20 geometry, and the exact `fak info` pane command) so an operator can preview the split before launching it.
rows.distinction: Not the live console renderTUI* panes or bench reports; this only previews the resolved guard-split plan as text for --split-dry-run.

rows.definition: maturity.RenderIssueResult is the human dry-run/live card for `fak maturity route`, summarizing the mode and the planned issue items.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderIssueResult renders the maturity-route planning card, not a benchmark document.

rows.definition: nightrun.RenderLedgerGapReport writes the benchmark-authority ledger-gap report to a writer: the authority's last-updated date, ledger totals, and the rows newer than or missing from the published benchmark surface.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports themselves; RenderLedgerGapReport renders the nightrun gap between the local ledger and the published benchmark authority.

rows.definition: supportmaturityscore.RenderReadOut renders the support-maturity readout rows as a deterministic ASCII table for the human `fak support` view.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderReadOut renders the support-maturity readout table for the support CLI.

rows.definition: cachevaluereport.RenderTwoTrack renders the cache-value profit-and-loss two-track report side by side (the WITNESSED reuse track and its OBSERVED-dollar projection) with the verdict.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown reports; RenderTwoTrack renders the cache-value P&L two-track view, not a benchmark comparison.
